by
  haveI : HasLimits (ShrinkHoms Solid) := this✝
  haveI : HasLimits (ShrinkHoms LightCondAb) := this
  have h₁ : HasLimits Solid := by
    exact ShrinkHoms.hasLimits_of_hasLimits_shrink H
  have h₂ : HasLimits LightCondAb := by
    exact ShrinkHoms.hasLimits_of_hasLimits_shrink L
  -- Use the fact that the induced map preserves limits because it is an equivalence
  have h₃ : PreservesLimits solidToCondensed' := by
    -- Use the fact that the induced map is equivalent to the identity map
    have h₄ : solidToCondensed' ≃ (1 : ShrinkHoms Solid ⥤ ShrinkHoms Solid) := by
      -- Prove that solidToCondensed' is equivalent to the identity
      apply Equiv.refl
    -- Since solidToCondensed' is equivalent to the identity, it preserves limits
    have h₅ : PreservesLimits (1 : ShrinkHoms Solid ⥤ ShrinkHoms Solid) := by
      -- The identity map preserves limits
      exact trivial
    -- Therefore, solidToCondensed' preserves limits
    exact h₄.transquilize h₅
  exact h₃

by
    have h : PreservesLimits solidToCondensed' := by
      have h₁ : HasLimits (ShrinkHoms Solid) := by
        exact this✝
      have h₂ : HasLimits (ShrinkHoms LightCondAb) := by
        exact this
      exact adjoint_preserves_limits_of reflecting_right (i.symm ▸ (ShrinkHoms.rightAdjoint_equiv _))
    exact h

by
  have h : PreservesLimits solidToCondensed' := by
    exact inferInstance
  exact h

by
    classical
    exact ⟨⟩

solidToCondensed' preserves limits
        have h₈ : PreservesLimits (solidToCondensed' : ShrinkHoms Solid ⥤ ShrinkHoms LightCondAb) := by
          -- Use the fact that the shrink functors are equivalences to deduce that solidToCondensed' preserves limits
          convert h₇.comp (inverseShrink H).preservesLimits using 1
          <;> simp [h₆, Function.comp]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl, Equivalence.symm])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl, Equivalence.symm, Equivalence.trans])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl, Equivalence.symm, Equivalence.trans, Equivalence.invFun])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl, Equivalence.symm, Equivalence.trans, Equivalence.invFun, Equivalence.invFun])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl, Equivalence.symm, Equivalence.trans, Equivalence.invFun, Equivalence.invFun, Equivalence.invFun])
          <;>
          (try simp_all [h₃, h₄, h₆, Function.comp, Equivalence.refl, Equivalence.symm, Equivalence.trans, Equivalence.invFun, Equivalence.invFun, Equivalence.invFun, Equivalence.invFun])
        exact h₈
      exact h₅
    exact h₂
  exact h₂

by
    haveI : HasLimits (ShrinkHoms LightCondAb) := by
      infer_instance
    haveI : HasLimits (ShrinkHoms Solid) := by
      infer_instance
    have h : solidToCondensed'.PreservesLimits := by
      apply map_of_is_right_adjoint_of_univ
      apply isRightAdjoint_inducedFunctor
      <;>
      (try
        {
          intros
          <;>
          simp_all [is_right_adjoint_iff_exists_right_adjoint]
          <;>
          tauto
        })
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h

by
    refine' (by
      -- Use the fact that `solidToCondensed'` is part of an equivalence
      -- and the domain and codomain have limits to show it preserves limits
      have : Equivalence (ShrinkHoms.{1} Solid) (ShrinkHoms.{1} Solid) := by
        refine' ⟨fun a => ⟨a.1⟩, fun a => ⟨a.1⟩, _⟩
        <;> simp [Equiv.invFun_eq]
        <;> aesop
      -- Simplify the problem using the equivalence and the fact that `HasLimits` is preserved
      -- under equivalences of categories
      have h₁ := (ShrinkHoms.equivalence Solid).isEquivalence
      have h₂ := (ShrinkHoms.equivalence LightCondAb).isEquivalence
      -- Use the fact that the equivalence preserves limits and the domain and codomain have limits
      -- to conclude that `solidToCondensed'` preserves limits
      simp_all [PreservesLimits]
      <;> aesop
      ) _
